ety: prob. S. ध्वस्त + तेल; cf. ḍhahnā
def: s.m. Scum of oil or ghī; oil in which anything has been fried.
adj. (f. -ī), Deceitful, tricky, knavish.

ety: prob. S. द्वन्द्व+क+कः; or fr. S. ध्वनिः
def: s.m. A kind of drum.

ety: the preceding, with S. इका, for कः
def: s.f. A small drum.
s.m. Subterfuge, trick, deception, cheating, chicanery; — adj. (dialec.), Tricky, artful, deceitful.

ety: dhandhlā+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: v.t. To trick, deceive, cajole, cheat.
s.m. = dhandhlā, q.v.
s.m. A wrangler, a creator of disturbance; a trickster, wily knave, cheat.

ety: prob. fr. S. धा by redupl.
def: v.t. To devour, bolt down, cram (oneself) with, to gormandize.
s.f. The scum of boiling sugar-cane juice.
s.m. 1˚ = ḍhanḍhorā; — 2˚ = ḍhanḍhoriyā, qq.v.

ety: prob. by redupl. fr. S. ढुण्ढ; cf. ḍhaḍhornā
def: s.m. Proclamation by beat of drum
subs: To proclaim by beat of drum.

def: s.m. Public crier, one who proclaims by beat of drum.
s.m. = dhandhlā, q.v.
s.m. = dhāṅdhlī, q.v.
s.m. = dhāṅdhlī, q.v.
s.m. = ḍhanḍhorā, q.v.
s.m. = ḍhaṅḍhoriyā, q.v.
s.m. = ḍhaṅḍhoriyā, q.v.
s.f. = dhāʼeṅ-dhāʼeṅ, q.v.s.v. dhāʼeṅ.
s.m. corr. of dhaneś or dhanes, q.v.
= dhaneś, q.v.s.v. dhan.

s.m. Mark, sign, characteristic, property, peculiarity (syn. lakshaṇ); method, mode, manner, fashion, way, style; form, figure, shape, build, make, mould; course of proceeding, practice, carriage, conduct, behaviour, manners, breeding; gracefulness, grace; accomplishment, art
subs: (-kā), To carry, or comport, oneself (in any particular manner or style), to behave; — to conform to a mode or fashion; to bend to circumstances.

def: s.f. A steep hill, precipice, cliff, high bank (of a river).

ety: ḍhang+Prk. अओ and इआ = S.अकः and इका
def: adj. Ill-formed, ill-fashioned, ill-shaped, shapeless, ugly, ill-favoured; ill-behaved, ill-mannered, ill-bred, unpolished, rude, uncouth, awkward, loutish; irregular, disordered, confused; untidy, slovenly
subs: s.m. Shapelessness; ill-manneredness, rudeness, uncouthness, awkwardness; disordered state; untidiness, &c.

def: s.m. A caste whose business it is to dig wells, tanks, &c.

ety: ḍhang+Prk. अओ and इआ = S.अकः and इका
def: adj. Ill-formed, ill-fashioned, ill-shaped, shapeless, ugly, ill-favoured; ill-behaved, ill-mannered, ill-bred, unpolished, rude, uncouth, awkward, loutish; irregular, disordered, confused; untidy, slovenly
subs: s.m. Shapelessness; ill-manneredness, rudeness, uncouthness, awkwardness; disordered state; untidiness, &c.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Well-shaped, comely, graceful, handsome; well-behaved.
s.f. (dialec.) = ḍhaṅg.
adj. Rice-growing, for the cultivation of rice (lands, or a country); — s.m. A cultivator of rice.
s.f. See s.v. dhan.
s.f. A kind of rice in the husk; — rice-land, good soil (fit for rice or other grain); — a light green colour.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Opulent, rich, wealthy, well off; — a rich man; — possessor, owner, proprietor, lord, master; husband; — a money-lender, creditor (cf. dhanik); an epithet of the Deity
subs: adj. Payable to the owner or to the person who purchases it (a hunḍī or bill); — s.m. Holder of a bill.

def: adj. &c. ( = dhan and dhanya, qq.v.
adj. Rich, wealthy, &c. ( = dhanī); pious, virtuous, excellent; — s.m. A rich man, &c. ( = dhanī); a creditor, money-lender; — a pious man.
s.f. The twenty-third lunar mansion (the dolphin; — it comprises four stars and is figured by a drum or tabor).
s.m. Coriander seed, Coriandrum sativum
subs: 'To give one a drink from the husk of a grain of coriander'; to tantalize, vex, disturb; to kill one by inches.

def: s.f. Lady, &c. ( = dhannī, q.v.).
s.m. = ḍhāk, q.v.

ety: prob. ḍhāṅk(nā)+ar = Prk. अडो = S. रः
def: s.m. A hedge, fence ( = ḍhān).
s.f. A rice-field; stiff soil on which rice is grown; a field which has been cropped with rice during the previous season.
s.f. A rice-field; stiff soil on which rice is grown; a field which has been cropped with rice during the previous season.
s.f. The season for cutting rice; — a kind of cloth.
s.f. = dhan-kar, q.v.

ety: ḍhāṅk˚ = Prk. ढक्क(इ) or ढक्के(इ) = S. स्थग्य(ते), pass. (used actively) of स्थग्; or perhaps S. स्थग्+कृ
def: v.t. To cover, to put a cover on; to shut, close; to conceal, hide (syn. ḍhāṅpnā)
= ḍhaknā, q.v.
s.f. = ḍhaknī, q.v.

ety: dhān, q.v.+kuṭṭī = Prk. कुट्टिआ = S. कुट्ट+इका
def: s.f. An instrument for pounding grain (especially rice) and separating it from the husk (consisting of a long beam with a vertical mallet at one end which falls on the grain, the other end being pressed down by the feet to raise the beam on its fulcrum).
adj. & s.m. See s.v. dhan, 'wealth.'

ety: prob. fr. S. धन्वन, rt. धन्व्; or by redupl. fr. S. धन्
def: v.n. To roll, reel, stagger, be unsteady; to be tossed about; to be upset; to spill (cf. ḍhalmalānā; ḍagmagānā).
adj. &c. See s.v. dhan = dhanya.
s.m. = dharnā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Fortunate, blest, successful, wealthy; true-speaking; — a fortunate or successful man; a rich man, a great banker.
s.f. A female owner, &c., proprietress, mistress, lady, wife (dhanā, dhaniyānī).
s.f. Beam, rafter.

ety: dhann˚, fr. dhannā ( = dharnā)+oṭā = S. पट्टकः or पत्त्रकः
def: s.m. A cross-beam (one of such as are used to sustain the supports of thatch).
s.m. corr. of ḍhaṅḍhoriyā, q.v.

ety: prob. a modification of ḍhāṅknā, q.v.; — or ḍhāṅp˚ = S. पिधा (अपि+धा)
def: v.t. To cover, put a cover on, to shut, close; to conceal, hide (cf. jhāṅpnā)
v.n. = ḍhapnā, q.v.
s.m. = dhvaṅs, q.v.
s.f. 1˚, Cough ( = dhāṅs, q.v.); — 2˚, Musquito ( = ḍāṅs, q.v.).

s.f. Irritation (in the throat) caused by inhaling or swallowing any pungent substance; coughing caused by any irritating or pungent substance (as chillies, pepper, &c.; e.g. mirćoṅ-kī dhāṅs ćaṛh-gaʼī); coughing, cough (particularly of horses or cattle = dhāṅsī).
s.m. Blame, accusation, false charge, calumny, defamation, undeserved abuse (cf. jhāṅsā)
subs: (-ko), To blame, accuse falsely, calumniate, defame, &c.

def: s.f. Coughing, a cough ( = dhāṅs).
v.n. To cough (through inhaling or swallowing any irritant).
v.n. = dhasnā, q.v.

ety: ḍhāṅs˚ = Prk. ढंस(इ) = S. दंश(ति), rt. दंश्
def: v.t. To blame, &c. ( = ḍhāṅsā denā, q.v.s.v. ḍhāṅsā; and cf. jhāṅsnā).
s.m. See s.v. dhan.
s.m. A rice field which has been cut.
s.f. = ḍhāṭī, q.v.
s.m. A rice field which has been cut.
s.f. = ḍhāṭī, q.v.
s.m. A bow; the sign Sagittarius of the Zodiac; — a measure of four cubits
subs: adj. Armed with a bow ( = dhanush-pāṇi)

def: s.m. A bowman, an archer; a watchman armed with a bow; name of a tribe of hill people who use bows and arrows (they are employed las archers, fowlers, house-guards, and in several menial occupations both of the house and of the field; while their women serve as midwives).
s.m. A bow; a rainbow; — fine narrow lace; embroidery; — a kind of covering or mantle (oṛhnī)
subs: s.f. The tingling or pricking sensation felt in a limb that is asleep; — tetanus
s.m. = dhanur-dhārī, and dhanuk-dhar, adj. & s.m. = dhanur-dhar, q.v.s.v. dhanus
s.f. A female archer; — a certain ceremony in honour of Śiva.

def: s.m. = dhanuk, dhanush, qq.v.
adj. Having the shape of a bow, bowed, arched.
s.f. The bow with which cotton is separated, or cleaned, or carded.
adj. & s.m. = ḍhām & ḍhāmā, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. dhanus.

ety: euphonically changed, in comp., to dhanur, and dhanush
def: s.m. A bow (for shooting); rainbow; a measure of length; arc of a circle; the sign Sagittarius of the zodiac (see dhanush, and dhanu)
subs: s.m. The tree Buchanania latifolia (syn. piyāl)
adj. (f. -ā), Bearing a bow, armed with a bow; — s.m. One armed with a bow, an archer, &c. ( = dhanur-dhārī); an epithet of Arjuna
s.f. A plant from the leaves of which a tough thread is extracted of which bow-strings were formerly made, Sanseviera zeylanica, or S. Roxburghiana
s.f. A plant from the leaves of which bow-strings were made (cf. dhanuh̥-śākhā).

ety: for dhanus, q.v.; but used in Hindī both in comp. and alone
def: s.m. A bow; rainbow, &c. ( = dhanuk)
subs: (or pānī), adj. & s.m. Armed with a bow; — an archer
s.m. Twang of a bow; — a severe spasmodic affection
s.f. Point or end of a bow
dhanush-mant, adj. & s.m. (f. matī), Having a bow; — an archer, bowman
vulg. dhanush-bān, s.m. Bow and arrow
s.m. Any religious ceremony in which the bow figures as an emblem.

def: s.m. A bowman, an archer ( = dhānuk, q.v.).
s.f. The plant Achyranthes aspera.
s.m. A bow.
s.m. A dry soil, a country scantily supplied with water, a desert, a waste; a firm spot, land, ground, shore
s.m. A thresher of rice; a seller of rice or grain.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. dhan.
adj. & s.m. = ḍhām & ḍhāmā, q.v.
= dhan-vān, q.v.s.v. dhan.

s.m. The physician of the gods (who was produced at the churning of the ocean holding a cup of amr̤it in his hands); a celebrated physician (also called Divo-dās, q.v.), who was king of Kāśi, and is considered to be the founder of the Hindū school of medicine (cf. dhanattar).

s.m. The physician of the gods (who was produced at the churning of the ocean holding a cup of amr̤it in his hands); a celebrated physician (also called Divo-dās, q.v.), who was king of Kāśi, and is considered to be the founder of the Hindū school of medicine (cf. dhanattar).
s.m. See s.v. dhanvā.

adj. & s.m. Armed with a bow; — cunning, sharp, shrewd; — an archer, a bowman; the sign Sagittarius; an epithet of Śiva, of Vishnu, and of Arjun; — a cunning or shrewd man, a sharp fellow, a wag, wit; — the plant Pentaptera (or Terminalia) Arjuna.
s.m. Grain, corn; rice, Oryza sativa; — coriander; — a measure equal to four sesamum seeds
subs: adj. Abounding in grain, rich in corn.

def: adj. Bringing wealth; fortunate, auspicious, well-fated, lucky, happy, blest; good, virtuous, worthy of greatness or glory; — intj. Well done! bravo! how fortunate! what happiness! blessings (on you)! — dhanya-bhāgya, vulg. dhan-bhāg, s.m. Good fortune, happy lot
subs: v.t. To call blessed; to pronounce blessings on, to bless
(-kā), To express devout thanks (for), to be very grateful (for)
Blessed is my lot! happy man am I! what a fortunate thing for me! — dhanya-vād, vulg. dhan-bād, s.m. Thanksgiving, thankfulness, thanks; praise, applause
(-kā), To express thankfulness or thanks (for); to give praise, to applaud (for)
s.m. One who gives utterance to thanks; one who praises or congratulates.

def: s.m. Coriander ( = dhaniyā).
s.f. Good fortune, fortunate condition.
s.m. The plant Grislea tomentosa (used in dyeing).

s.f. The distance a man can run without taking breath, a distance of about half a mile; distance, space, range ( = dhappā); a large expanse of low ground; a ghāṭ or passage; a foot (measure of length).
s.m. Sound, noise, crack; clack (of a shoe in walking), noise of a heavy body falling, thud; thump, blow ( = dhappā, q.v.).
s.m. A large tabor or drum.
s.m. = ḍaphālī, ḍafālī, qq.v.
v.t. = dhāpnā, q.v.
s.m. Running, race.
s.m. (dialec.) = ḍhapnā, q.v.
v.t. To beat or play upon (a drum, &c.), to drum on (a table, &c.)
s.m. = ḍhap, q.v.
s.f. A short run, a short kos, a short distance or range.

ety: dhapiyā˚ = dhappī (fem. of dhappā, q.v.)+ā = āw = Prk. āwa, or āwe = S. āpi, caus. aug.
def: v.t. To slap, thump, cuff, box, &c. ( = dhappā marnā).

ety: dhāp˚ = S. धापय(ति), fr. caus. of rt. धे
def: v.t. To fill, satisfy, satiate; — v.n. To be filled, be sated; to be weary ( = dhapnā).
v.t. = ḍhāṅpnā, q.v.
v.n. To be covered; to be concealed; to lie hidden, to hide; to be submerged; to sink; — s.m. Covering; cover, lid.
v.n. To be filled, be sated; to be weary (of), be sick (of).
s.f. dim. of ḍhapnā, q.v.
s.m. Distance, range, space, &c. ( = dhāp, q.v.).
s.m. A stain, blot, &c. ( = dhabbā, q.v.); — a slap, box, thump, blow (with the flat of the hand); — deception, cheating; misfortune, loss, damage
subs: v.n. To get a thump or blow; to suffer loss
v.t. To slap, thump; to damage; to deceive, cheat.

def: adj. = ḍhappū, q.v.

ety: prob. S. स्तम्ब+कः (with w inserted); cf. ḍhabbū, ḍhep
def: adj. & s.m. Podgy, bulky, fat, corpulent; — a podgy man, &c.
s.m. = dhaṛ, q.v.; — adj. = dahaṛ or dhaṛ, q.v.

adj. Holding, bearing, carrying; wearing; possessing, having, possessed of; containing; keeping; sustaining, preserving (used as last member of Hindī compounds); — s.m. A rope, line; the earth ( = dharā; dhartī).
s.m. Stream, &c. ( = dhār; used in comp.)
s.m. Trunk (of the body), the body; a body, party, side; — (dialec.) side, face; direction; — a kind of drum (sounded in the arena when a wrestler is successful)
subs: s.m. One of a party or side, a partisan
s.m. 'Breaking the back,' a trick in wrestling
adj. & s.m. Broken-backed; hunch-backed; — a broken-backed person; a hunch-back
v.n. The body to be deprived of power or motion, &c., to be paralyzed
dhaṛ-meṅ paṛnā, v.n. 'To be taken into the trunk or belly,' to be stowed away, be swallowed, be gulped
v.t. To take down, to swallow, gulp, to stow away, make away with.

def: adj. & s.m. Firm, hard, strong, stout, compact, solid; intense, excessive, severe, violent, fierce, furious; — firmly, tightly, compactly; fearlessly, right forward, decidedly, plainly, flatly; violently, noisily; — s.f. A noisy fall or blow, a thump, bump, thud, &c.
subs: s.f. A smart or sharp succession of sounds (as of falling bodies, blows, firing, &c.), rattling, thumping, banging, &c.; — adv. With a loud and continued noise, bang-bang, rattle, thump-thump, crash-crash, thud-thud
s.f. A smart succession of noises, quick or repeated hammering or thumping, &c. (see dhaṛ-ā-dhaṛ); report (of fire-arms), crack, crash, explosion
adv. Intensely, violently, fiercely, furiously, noisily, with a crack, or crash, &c.; — s.f. Intense or repeated noise or rattling, &c.; knock, rap (at a door, &c.), rat-tat; shaking, shivering
v.n. To burn furiously, to blaze
v.n. To make a rattling noise, &c., to knock (at a door); to make a noise (with a drum), to beat (a drum)
v.n. To shake or shiver violently.

def: s.f. A kind of ear-ring.
= ḍhāl, q.v.

r: dhāṛ, dhāṛā, dhāṛnā, dhāṛ paṛnā, par, dhāṛ denā, dhāṛ mārnā
def: s.f. = H دهاڙا धाड़ा dhāṛā, s.m. (fr. dhāṛnā, q.v.), A multitude, crowd, band, gang; a band or gang of robbers, banditti; — a robbery; — a stream or body of water falling from a height, a torrent, a water-fall
subs: (-par), A band of robbers to fall (upon), to be ataacked by a band of robbers
dhāṛ mārnā, To plunder in bands.

def: s.m. A heap (of corn, &c.).

s.f. A continuous line, a line, lineament; a line or chain (of mountains, &c.); edge (of a sword, &c.); sharpness; — flowing, flow, stream, current; a line or stream of descending fluid (as milk from a cow, &c.; hence), a stream of milk, milk; a libation
subs: s.f. An even or general distribution of charges, rates, &c.
(or pe) mārnā, v.t. To disregard, contemn, despise, scorn (cf. dhār-mārnā)
adj. & s.m. Edged, sharp; — an edged or sharp tool or weapon
or dhār-dhūrā, s.m. Boundary (between two villages); boundary formed by a stream
(-par, or -kī), To give an edge (to), to sharpen; — to winnow
To give a stream of milk, to give milk
v.n. The edge to be turned or to become jagged
(-kī), To receive, or to drink, milk as it streams from the teat
(-par), To pass urine, to make water (on); to despise, scorn, &c. (see dhār-par māṛnā)
(-kī), To milk; — to produce an edge (on), to sharpen.

def: s.f. A line of descending fluid, a stream, current (of water, or of a river, &c.), a channel, a water-course; a torrent; a flood; a hard shower (of rain); a drop; — margin or border (of anything); circumference or periphery (of a wheel); — sharp edge (of any tool or weapon); — a multitude
subs: adv. In a stream, in streams
(˚rā+ās˚), s.m. A heavy shower; a large drop (of rain)

def: s.m. A body, band, party, side, faction; — a weight (usually of ten ser), or the quantity weighed by it; a counter-balancing weight; a large weight made up by counter-balancing stones, &c. against a small weight, and adding them to that weight, and so on, till the original weight is doubled, or quadrupled, or any required standard of weight is obtained
subs: (-kā), To weigh (by means of a dhaṛā)
v.n. To make up a party or side; — to make up a standard to weigh with (see dhaṛā)
or paṛnā, v.n. To be split up into bands, or parties, or factions
To frame or bring a counter charge.

r: dhāṛā, dhāṛ, dhāṛnā, dhāṛ paṛnā, par, dhāṛ denā, dhāṛ mārnā
def: s.m. = H دهاڙ धाड़ dhāṛ, s.f. (fr. dhāṛnā, q.v.), A multitude, crowd, band, gang; a band or gang of robbers, banditti; — a robbery; — a stream or body of water falling from a height, a torrent, a water-fall
subs: (-par), A band of robbers to fall (upon), to be ataacked by a band of robbers
dhāṛ mārnā, To plunder in bands.

def: s.f. The earth
subs: s.m. Earth, ground; surface, superficies, table
s.m. 'God of the earth,' an epithet of a Brāhman.

def: adv. & s.f. = H دهڙادهڙي धड़ाधड़ी dhaṛā-dhaṛī, s.f. See s.v. dhaṛ.
s.f. = H دهڙادهڙ धड़ाधड़ dharā-dhaṛ, adv. & s.f. See s.v. dhaṛ.

ety: dharā, i.q. dharāʼo, q.v.+har = Prk. घरं = S. गृहं
def: s.m. A depository.
s.f. Placing, putting down; holding, laying hold of, seizing.
adj. & s.m. Holding, containing; — what or who holds, a receptacle; a debtor.
s.f. Beating, throbbing, palpitating; beat, throb, palpitation; trepidation, fear, alarm (cf. thalak).
s.m. A loud report, explosion, crash, peal, raging (of a tempest), violence (of an attack, &c.), din and roar (of a battle); vehemence; rapidity; boldness
subs: adv. With a loud report, with a crash; boldly, fearlessly; rapidly, quickly.

def: s.m. the Urdū form of the next, q.v.
s.m. A scarecrow ( = ḍhaṛkhā, q.v.).
s.m. Report, explosion, crash ( = dhaṛākā, q.v.); sound of hammering, &c.; alarming, frightening, intimidating, bullying; bravado (cf. dharsh); — a crowd ( = dhaṛallā; bhīṛ).
v.n. = dhaṛaknā, q.v.

ety: dhaṛak˚ = Prk. दड्ढक्क(इ), fr. S. दग्ध(p.p. of rt. दह्)+कृ
def: v.n. To blaze up, to burn fiercely; to be intensely hot (as the body in fever, &c.); to be inflamed (with anger, or lust, &c.; cf. bhaṛaknā); — to be distressed, to tremble (from fear, &c.); to beat, throb, palpitate, flutter.
v.t. = ḍhalaknā, q.v.
s.m. Sound of beating or hammering, &c. ( = dhaṛakkā, q.v.); — a crowd, band, host, swarm ( = bhīṛ); — a scarecrow.
adv. With a crash, &c.

s.f. The earth ( = dharanī); the womb; the navel, the umbilical cord or vein (supposed by Hindū physicians to become occasionally displaced, and thus to give rise to various morbid symptoms); — a beam; — accent, tone
subs: dharan ḍignā or haṭnā, The womb, or the navel, to be displaced.

def: s.m. Holding, bearing, carrying, keeping, wearing; upholding, maintaining, sustaining, preserving; possessing, having, assuming; knowing, acquiring; undertaking
subs: v.t. To bear; to possess; to wear; to assume; to seize, retain; to draw, to absorb, &c.

def: s.m. Holding, seizing; having, possessing, keeping; bearing, supporting; placing; fastening, &c. (see dharnā); — a support; a fastening; a bank, dike, dam; — the female breast; — a sort of measure or weight.

s.f. Keeping in remembrance, retaining in the mind; a good memory, memory; — continuance in rectitude, keeping in the right way, fortitude, firmness, steadiness, resolution; aptitude to acquire (wisdom, &c.).
v.t. To owe ( = dhārnā); — to deposit, to give in deposit ( = dharnā); — v.n. (prov.) To be laid hold of, be seized, be appre hended, be imprisoned.
s.m. Body and limbs; — (dialec.) adj. Large, lusty, stalwart, strapping
subs: 'naked in body and limb,' stark naked).

def: s.f. The earth, globe; earth, soil, land, ground; — a beam
subs: s.m. lit. 'Bearing or sustaining the earth'; an epithet of Śesh-nāg, the serpent which is said to uphold the earth; — an epithet of Vishnu in the form of a tortoise and a boar; — a mountain
s.f. 'Daughter of the earth,' an epithet of Sītā wife of Rāma (as produced from the soil by Janaka when he was ploughing a spot to prepare it for sacrifice)
s.m. An esculent root or bulb.

def: s.f. A straight line; any tubular vessel of the body.
s.m. = dharāʼī, q.v.
s.f. (m. ?) = ḍhāras, q.v.
s.f.m.(?), Firmness of mind, confidence, courage, boldness, heart, encouragement, comfort; fortitude, patience (cf. dhīraj)
subs: To keep up the spirits, to take heart, have courage, or patience, &c.
(-kī), or ḍhāṛas denā (-ko), To impart courage (to), encourage, animate, cheer, comfort.

def: = ḍhāṛas, q.v.
v.t. To overpower; to repress, restrain, curb, chide.
s.f. Ascertainment by guess or conjecture.
s.m. = dharāʼonā, q.v.
s.f. Land ascertained and apportioned by estimate (not by measurement).
s.f. A woman married a second time; second marriage of a girl.

ety: dhar, prob. rt., or past. conj. part., of dharnā, or = dhaṛ, q.v.; and dhamaknā, q.v.
def: v.n. To come or move on rapidly, to proceed with tumultuous rapidity, to rush off or forward, to set off in hot haste, to start off (e.g. dhar-dhamkā wahāṅ se laṛtā-huʼā shahr-kī t̤araf. — Saudā).
adv. & s.f. See s.v. dhaṛ.
v.n. To knock (at a door); to sound (a drum), make a rattling or banging noise; — to flutter, to beat, to palpitate.

ety: dharā, p. p. of dharnā+Prk. इल्ली or इली = S. इली
def: s.f. (dialec.) A kept woman, a mistress, a concubine.
s.f. = ḍhāṛas, q.v.

ety: prob. fr. S. धृष्ट, rt. धृ
def: s.m. A caste of (Mohammadan) singers (among the retainers of kings and great men, whom they precede on their march with armies, or in processions, chanting praises, &c.); an individual of that caste; a singer, a musician.
s.f. = ḍhāṛin, q.v.

adj. & s.m. Bearing, holding, carrying, wearing, having, keeping, possessing; sustaining, supporting, maintaining, preserving, &c.; — bearer, holder, &c. (used as last member of comp., e.g. jīv-dhārī).
s.f. The plant Lythrum fructicosum (used in dyeing); the plant Grislea tomentosa ( = dhātakī).
s.f. A line; border or edge (of cloth, &c.)
subs: (with loc.), To mark lines (on the teeth) with missī, to apply missī (to the teeth or lips).

def: s.m. = ḍhāṛī, q.v.
s.f. A weight of five ser or seers; the quantity weighed at a time, five seers; — a large weight made up, &c. (see dhaṛā); — five hundred rupees
subs: To spoil (one) of everything, to make a clean sweep (of).

ety: prob. fr. S. धृष्ट, rt. धृ
def: s.m. A caste of (Mohammadan) singers (among the retainers of kings and great men, whom they precede on their march with armies, or in processions, chanting praises, &c.); an individual of that caste; a singer, a musician.
s.f. A line; lineament; a stripe, a streak; a groove; — a small buttress; an army, a host; — margin, extremity, border

s.m. The second husband of a Hindū widow among the lower classes (this connexion being contrary to the spirit of the Hindū institutions, is formed without any particular ceremony, except marking the bride's head with minium).
s.f. A woman of the ḍhāṛī caste; the wife of a ḍhāṛī.
s.m. (local), A kind of rice.
s.m. Name of a subordinate Hindū caste.
part. Borne, supported, upheld, maintained, sustained; assumed; — s.m. A horse's foot.
s.f. A female bearer, supporter; the earth.
s.m. Beating, throb, &c. ( = dhaṛak); suspense, doubt, fear, apprehension; — crash, peal; thunder; shock, blow (cf. dhakkā, dhaćkā).
s.f. = dhaṛak, q.v.
v.t. = ḍhalkānā, q.v.
v.t. To cause to beat or palpitate, to put in a state of trepidation, to frighten, alarm.

ety: Prk. डड्ढकाअओ = S. दग्धकाक+कः; cf. daheṛ
def: s.m. A raven.
s.m. Trepidation, alarm ( = dhaṛkā); — a scarecrow ( = dhaṛak-kā, q.v.).
s.m. A scarecrow ( = dhokhā).
s.m. A scarecrow ( = ḍharkhā).

r: dharma, dharm, dharam, lit, dharmāt, dharmātmā, ˚ma, āt˚
def: s.m.f. Religious, pious, virtuous, just; — a pious or virtuous person, a saint; — the Holy Spirit; — s.f. Justice
subs: s.m. The observance of religion or of prescribed duty, &c. (whether ceremonial or otherwise); religiousness of behaviour; — dharm-āćār or dharm-āćārī, s.m. (f. -ini), One who practises the principles or the ceremonies of religion, &c. (see dharm-ćārī)
(˚ma+adh˚), s.m. Religion and irreligion; right and wrong; justice and injustice
s.f. lit. 'Test of right and wrong'; a kind of ordeal by drawing lots or slips of black and white paper
vulg. dharm-adhikār (˚ma+adh˚), s.m. The administration of justice; the right to administer law; the office of a judge
vulg. dharm-adhikārī, and dharmādhyaksh (˚ma+adh˚), vulg. dharmādhīksh, s.m. Minister of justice, chief officer of justice, an administrator of the law, a judge, magistrate
(˚ma+ar˚), adv. For the sake of justice or religion, for religious purposes; — s.m. A charitable grant, religious endowment
(˚ma+ar˚), s.m. One who is jealous for justice or religion
(˚ma+ar˚), s.m. A sacred grove or wood; name of a sacred forest in Madhyadeś into which Dharma is said to have retired
(˚ma+ās˚), s.m. Throne of justice, judgment seat, the bench
adj. & s.m. Having a passion for religion, zealous, enthusiastic in religious matters; — a religious zealot or enthusiast
(˚ma+an˚), s.m. Conformity to law or virtue; course or practice of duty; — adv. In conformity to law, &c., according to law or duty &c.
vulg. dharm-autār (˚ma+av˚), s.m. Incarnation of Dharma, or god of justice (an epithet of respect for a just or upright man); your holiness; your excellency; his holiness, &c.
v.n. To swear by (one's) creed, to take an oath
adj. Contrary to law or justice, opposed to religion or duty, unlawful or unjust; impious, immoral
s.f. A virtuous and amiable wife
s.m. A just or virtuous man
adj. Cherishing the observance of the law, observing the law, dutiful
s.m. A deed of gift or endowment for a religious purpose; — the glomerous fig-tree, Ficus glomerata
s.m. A lawful son, a son begot from a sense of duty; 'son of the god Dharma,' an epithet of the Pānḍu prince Yudishṭhir
s.m. One who, in the name of religion, exercises care for a defenceless widow; a lawfully married man; a man who has a first and only wife
s.f. A lawful wife (one lawfully married and of the same caste as her husband)
adj. & s.m. Eminent in piety; — one who is eminent in piety
s.m. A seeker after dharm, q.v.; — adv. For the sake of establishing religion, &c.
s.m. Holy Scripture (as the Bible, the Qorʼān, &c.)
s.f. 'The scales of religion'; weighing or considering religion for the purpose of enquiry
s.f. Abandonment of religion, apostasy; abandonment of virtue, or duty, &c.
vulg. dharm-gyān, s.m. The knowledge of moral, legal, and religious duty
vulg. dharma-gya, adj. Knowing the law or what is right, knowing (one's) duty, conversant with virtue
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Observing the law, fulfilling (one's) duties, practising virtue, virtuous, dutiful, moral; — one who is observant of religion or duty, &c.; an honest spouse
s.f. A dutiful and virtuous wife
s.m., or dharm-ćintā, s.f. Pondering on the law or duty, study of virtue, consideration of moral duties, virtuous reflection
s.m. lit. 'Slave of duty,' &c.; an attendant upon a temple
s.m. Violation of law or right, injustice, oppression
adj. & s.m. Violating the law, or right, &c., wicked, immoral; — a wicked or unjust man, an infidel; a villain
s.m. An epithet of the chief light of a religion; e.g. Christ, Rāma, Moḥammad, &c.
s.m. A messenger in the cause of religion
adj. & s.m. Supporting or maintaining justice or the law, &c., virtuous, just, moral; — a maintainer of justice, &c.
adj. & s.m. Displaying the flag or colours of religion; — one who makes a livelihood by assumed devotion, a religious hypocrite or impostor
s.m. A mass or accumulation of dharm, q.v.

dharm-rāʼe, s.m. 'King of justice,' just king; an epithet of Yama or Pluto, and of Yudhishṭhir, and of Arjun; — a king (in general); (for dharm-rājya), a kingdom where justice is administered; ruling justly
s.f. Fostering religion, or law, &c.; zeal for religion (whether ceremonial or otherwise)
s.m. A guardian or patron of law, or justice, or religion
dharm-rūpī, adj. Having the form of virtue, &c., consisting of, or replete with, virtue; virtuous, righteous
adj. Obstructing the law, opposed to law or virtue, illegal, immoral; — a law-breaker, &c.
s.f. A court of justice, tribunal; an assembly of pious persons; a religious society
s.m. 'Pillar of righteousness'; an epithet of a very religious man
s.m. 'Place of justice'; name of a town
adj. & s.m. Of or relating to religion, &c.; — anything inherent in or relating to religion
s.f. A code or collection of laws (especially the work of some saint or divine person, as Manu, &c.)
adv. In good faith, honestly; solemnly
s.m. 'Barrier of justice'; an epithet of Śiva; name of a son of Aryak
s.m. A body or code of laws (esp. the code of Hindū law), jurisprudence
adj. Of or relating to the code of Hindū law; lawful

s.f. A court of justice; an edifice erected for pious purposes, a charitable institution, hospital, alms-house, monastery, a place of rest for travellers and pilgrims; — dharm-sīl, adj. Of a virtuous disposition, virtuous, just, pious
s.f. or dharm-śīlatva, s.m. Piety of disposition; virtuousness
adj. (f. -ī), Adopted by law; of or relating to law

s.m., or dharm-kārya, s.m., or dharm-kriyā (vulg. kiryā), s.f., or dharm-karm, s.m. A religious or virtuous act, work of merit, religious duty or exercise, any indispensable act of religion; righteous conduct, acting according to law
To perform a duty; to do justice; to practise virtue, do good; to give alms
s.m. The department or province of the law; — name of a plain in the north-west of India near Dehli (the scene of the great battle between the Kurus and Pāṇḍus, commonly called Kurukshetr)
v.n. To store up treasure in the future world by good works and a virtuous life, to work out (one's) salvation
s.m. The book of the law, the sacred scriptures (as the Bible, the Vedas, the Qorʼān, &c.; syn. dharm-pustak)
s.m. Observance of moral or religious precepts, accepting or following the law
s.m. = dharma-jnān, q.v.; — dharm-mūrtti, dharam-mūrat, s.m. 'Image of righteousness,' an epithet of respect (used by Brāhmans, &c. in addressing a Rājā or a Vaiśya)
s.m. The foundation of Hindū law and religion; the Vedas
adj. Consisting of law or virtue, made up or replete with virtue; moral, righteous
s.m. Attachment to virtue or religion, piety, virtue
adj. Attached to virtue; engaged in religious duties; pious, holy
s.f. Fulfilment or discharge of duty, moral or religious observance; duty
s.f. Firmness in religion; steadfastness in virtue
adj. Discussing the law or duty; contending or talking about religion
adj. Endowed with virtue, virtuous, pious, upright, just, lawful
vulg. dharm-upadeś (˚ma+up˚), s.m. Instruction in law or duty; a discourse on religion; moral or religious instruction
vulg. dharm-upadeśak, s.m. A teacher of the law, a gurū or spiritual preceptor
s.m. Contention or dispute about religion
s.m. A legal marriage (it is of five kinds, viz. Brāhma, Daiva, Arsha, Gandharb, and Prājapatya)
s.m. One who is acquainted with the law and religion
adj. Devoid of religion or virtue, unrighteous, faithless, worthless
s.m. A religious war, a crusade

def: s.m. A kind of snake ( = dhāman, q.v.); a species of tree.
= dharm-ātmā, q.v.s.v. dharm.
s.f. = S دهرمتو धर्मत्व dharmatva, s.m. Religion, virtue, righteousness, rectitude, probity, morality, piety; — inherent nature, peculiar property.
s.m. = S دهرمتا धर्मता dharmatā, s.f. Religion, virtue, righteousness, rectitude, probity, morality, piety; — inherent nature, peculiar property.
adj. & s.m. Obeying the law; knowing (one's) duties; virtuous, just, upright, religious, pious, holy, godly; — a virtuous or just man, a pious or holy man.
adj. Righteous, just, virtuous, religious, pious, devout; faithful.
s.f. = S دهارمکتو धार्मिकत्व dhārmikatva, s.m. Righteousness, justice, virtuousness, &c.
s.m. = S دهارمکتا धार्मिकता dhārmikatā, s.f. Righteousness, justice, virtuousness, &c.
adj. & s.m. Completely harmonizing or agreeing with law or virtue; very virtuous or pious; — a very virtuous or pious person.
adj. & s.m. Completely harmonizing or agreeing with law or virtue; very virtuous or pious; — a very virtuous or pious person.

ety: dhār˚ = Prk. धर(इ), or धरे(इ) = S. धारय(ति), caus. of rt. धृ
def: v.t. To hold, bear, carry; to have, keep; to uphold, support, sustain; — to owe; — to pour (water).

ety: dhār˚ = Prk. धार(इ), or धारे(इ), fr. S. धार
def: v.t. To pour (water, &c.).

ety: dhar˚ = Prk. धर(इ) = S. धर(ते) 'place,' and धर(ति), 'seize,ʼrt. धृ
def: v.t. To place, put, put down, deposit, lay, lay down; to pledge, stake; to put on, apply; to put (to), apply (the mind, or ear, &c.), to keep (at); — to have (in the hand), to hold, lay hold of, clutch, grasp, gripe, seize, to hold fast or pertinaciously; to take; to assume; to keep, retain; to catch, seize, apprehend, give in charge; to take (a side), espouse (a cause), entertain or hold (an opinion); to have, possess, hold, own, occupy, take possession of; to acquire, receive, obtain
subs: v.t. (intens.), To put down, set or lay down; to pledge, stake, &c. ( = dharnā)
v.t. To put aside, put by, lay by; to keep, retain, take care of
v.t. (intens.) To lay hold of, take hold of, grasp, seize ( = dharnā)
v.t. To drag off or away by main force; to pull or haul away.

def: s.m. The act of sitting in restraint at the door of a debtor, by the creditor or his agent, to enforce the payment of a debt; sitting doggedly and fasting at the door of a temple, &c., to extort favours from the idol, &c., or to obtain compliance with some demand
subs: dhaṛnā denā, v.n. To enforce payment of a debt or compliance with a demand by sitting (or seating an agent) doggedly at the debtor's door; to extort favours from an idol, &c. by sitting doggedly and fasting at the door of a temple, &c.

def: v.t. = ḍhālnā, q.v.

ety: dhāṛ˚ = Prk. धाड(इ) = S. ध्राड(ते), rt. ध्राड् (adopted fr. the Prk.; prob. a denom. of ध्रष्ट p.p.p. of ध्रज् 'to glide, flow)
def: v.t. To send forth, pour forth, pour out; to shed, &c. to cast (metal in fusion), to mould, &c. (see ḍhālnā, the more com. form); — to roar, to bellow, to thunder (in this sense usually dahāṛnā; cf. ḍakārnā).
v.n. To flow down, to come down; to come; to draw near, approach.

ety: dharan (i.q. dharnā)+ait = Prk. āʼitto = āyanto = āwento = S. āpayan, the term. of the pres. part. with the caus. augment āpi
def: s.m. One who sits in restraint at a person's door to compel payment of a debt or compliance with a demand; a dun (see dharnā, and dharnā denā).
s.m. = next, q.v.

ety: dharoh˚, prob. = dharāʼo, q.v.+ar or aṛ = Prk. अडी = S.अ+र+ई
def: s.f. What is committed to the charge (of another, syn. amānat), charge, trust; deposit, pledge, pawn; — adv. As a trust, or deposit
subs: (or dharoṛ) rakhnā, v.t. To commit to the charge (of), to place in trust, to deposit.

ety: dharoh˚, prob. = dharāʼo, q.v.+ar or aṛ = Prk. अडी = S.अ+र+ई
def: s.f. What is committed to the charge (of another, syn. amānat), charge, trust; deposit, pledge, pawn; — adv. As a trust, or deposit
subs: (or dharoṛ) rakhnā, v.t. To commit to the charge (of), to place in trust, to deposit.

def: s.f. = dharohar, q.v.
s.f. contrac. of dharohaṛ, q.v.
s.f.(m.?) = dharohar, q.v.

ety: dhar(nā)+aut = āwat = Prk. आवती = S. आप्ति (i.e. आपि caus. augment+aff. ति)
def: s.f. = dharohaṛ, q.v.

ety: past. conj. part. of dharnā and pakaṛnā
def: adv. By main force, by force.
s.m. Repressing, curbing, overcoming; overbearing conduct, violence.
s.m. = dhartā, q.v.
s.m. Discount, commission.
s.m. Holder, bearer, supporter, preserver; a debtor.
adj. To be held; to be upheld, be supported; to be had, be possessed; to be placed, be fixed.
s.f. The earth; earth, ground, land, soil, mould
subs: dhartī bāhnā or jotnā or ćīrnā
To plough the earth or land
On the earth's surface, on the earth

def: s.m. A species of mainā (bird).
s.f. = dhaṛwā, q.v.
s.m. A bird of the mainā kind ( = dhaṛuʼā).
s.m. A paṭwārī, or market officer, whose business is to weigh the grain, &c. brought into a market, or at the harvest (and whose perquisite is a dhaṛī, q.v.).
v.t. To cause to be placed, &c.; to cause to be held or laid hold of, to have one seized, or apprehended, or given in charge, &c. (see dharnā).

(fr. dhasnā, q.v.), s.m.(f.?) A steep slope (as of a river's bank, or the side of a hill), any declivous place; glacis (of a fort); a battery, earthworks, fortification; a sterile sandy eminence; (local) red sterile soil.
s.f. Giving way, sinking; coughing, cough ( = dhāṅs).

ety: dhasak˚ = Prk. धसक्क(इ), fr. S. ध्वंस+कृ
def: v.n. To give way, to slip (out of place); to sink (as a slough); to yield, to slip or fall down, give way (as a mud wall); to enter, pierce, stick (into); — to cough.
s.f.(m.?) A slough, &c. ( = dhasan, dhasān, q.v.).

ety: S. दशार्णा; but considered by some as identical with dhasān following
def: s.f. Name of a river which rises in the Vindhya hills, flows through Bundelkhanḍ, and falls into the Betwā (a tributary of the Jamnā).
s.f. The state of being thrust into; — adj. & s.f. = dhasān, q.v.
( = dhasan), adj. & s.f.(m.?) Having quicksands, swampy, boggy; — a quicksand, swamp, bog, slough, quagmire.
v.t. To cause to sink (into, -meṅ), to cause to enter or pierce, to thrust or drive (into), to stick (into)
s.m. A slough, &c. ( = dhasān, q.v.).
s.m. (fr. dhasaknā, q.v.) = dhasak.

ety: dhas˚ = Prk. धंस(इ) or धस(इ) = S. ध्वंम(ते), rt. ध्वंस्
def: v.n. To sink (into, -meṅ, as into a slough); to enter, run (into), stick (into), pierce; to be pierced, be stuck (into), be thrust (into); to yield, give way
s.m. A mineral, fossil, metal, ore, &c. (see dhātu); — s.f. Semen virile
s.f. A habit; a passion, mania; a bad habit, vice
subs: dhat paṛnā. v.n. A habit to be acquired
or to grow (on one), to become addicted (to).

def: s.f. A word used to encourage elephants, or to stop them, &c.
subs: intj. Stop! stand! halt! lie down!

def: s.m. One who places or lays, a founder, maker, creator, originator, author; supporter, maintainer, preserver, sustainer, nourisher, parent; name of Brahmā; name of one of the Ádityas
subs: s.m. Name of Brahmā; name of two of the deities.

ety: prob. S. धर्तृ, धर्ता; rt. धृ
def: s.m. A handkerchief or cloth tied over the ears and head (to keep the beard up).
s.m. The act of driving away; — putting off, evasion
subs: v.t. To drive away, turn out; to put off.

def: s.f. The tree Grislea tomentosa.
s.f. Binding tight, stretching on the rack; rack, torment, torture; — a noose or gin fastened on a horse's nose; nose-string (of an ox, or camel, &c.; syn. nakel)
subs: or denā, or lagānā (-kī, or -ko), To fasten a noose or gin on the mouth or nose (of a horse, &c.).

def: s.f. A rope or cord used instead of a bridle, a nose-string (syn. bāg-ḍor; nakel).
s.f. Advancing towards an enemy, confronting or assaulting an enemy, assault.
s.f. Binding tight, stretching on the rack; rack, torment, torture; — a noose or gin fastened on a horse's nose; nose-string (of an ox, or camel, &c.; syn. nakel)
subs: or denā, or lagānā (-kī, or -ko), To fasten a noose or gin on the mouth or nose (of a horse, &c.).

def: adj. & s.m. Sturdy, lusty, bulky; bold, overbearing; base, low, ignoble, spurious; — a lusty fellow, a great hulk, &c. (see ḍhaṭīngṛā).
adj. & s.m. Sturdy, burly, &c. ( = dhaṭīṅgar, q.v.); — a big burly fellow, a great hulk of a man.
adj. & s.m. Sturdy, burly, &c. ( = dhaṭīṅgar, q.v.); — a big burly fellow, a great hulk of a man.
s.f. A rope or cord used instead of a bridle, a nose-string (syn. bāg-ḍor; nakel).

ety: dhat, 'a habit, q.v. + Prk. इओ and अल्लो = S. इकः and अलः
def: adj. & s.m. Having an inveterate habit, addicted to; — one who is addicted to any vice.

ety: dhat, 'a habit, q.v. + Prk. इओ and अल्लो = S. इकः and अलः
def: adj. & s.m. Having an inveterate habit, addicted to; — one who is addicted to any vice.
s.m. = dhātā, q.v.
s.f. A wet-nurse, a foster-mother, nurse, mother ( = dāʼī); the earth; — emblic myrobalan, Emblica officinalis.
s.f. An epithet of Sarasvatī.
s.m. Emblic myrobalan, the plant Emblica officinalis.
s.m. 1˚ = ḍaṭṭā or ḍāṭ, qq.v.; — 2˚ = ḍhaṭhiyā, q.v.
s.f. A rope or cord used instead of a bridle, a nose-string (syn. bāg-ḍor; nakel).

ety: dhat, 'a habit, q.v. + Prk. इओ and अल्लो = S. इकः and अलः
def: adj. & s.m. Having an inveterate habit, addicted to; — one who is addicted to any vice.

s.m. An element, primary or elementary substance (viz. earth, fire, water, air, atmosphere); constituent element of the body; primary or essential fluid or juice, a secretion; semen; the matter which oozes in a gleet; a humour or affection of the body (as phlegm, wind, and bile); any one of the five qualities or properties of the elements observed by the organs of sense (viz. sound, tangibility, colour, flavour, smell); a primary element of the earth or of mountains, a mineral, fossil, metal, ore; the primary element of words, root, crude form, radical, verbal root
subs: adj. Promoting the elementary juices or secretions
vulg. dhātup, s.m. The alimentary juice, chyle
s.m. 'Chief of minerals,' green sulphate of iron, green vitriol
s.m. An assayer; a miner; — a mineralogist, metallurgist
s.m. 'Friend of metals'; borax (used as a flux)

def: s.m. See s.v. dhātu.
s.m. The thorn-apple (a powerful narcotic), Datura alba, or D. fastuosa (see below)
subs: (-ko), To administer dhatūrā (to), to drug or poison with dhatūrā

def: s.m. One who drugs or poisons with dhatūrā; — a cheat, knave, impostor.
s.f. The tree Grislea tomentosa, Roxb., or Lythrum fruticosum, Linn., and its wood.
s.f. The iron band of a wheel, the strake or tire.
s.m. (dialec.), Deep water, &c. ( = dah, q.v.).
s.m. (dialec.), Worry, annoyance, bother.
= dhauṅknā, q.v.
s.f. = dhauṅknī, q.v.

ety: S. धाव (rt. धाव)+al = Prk. -अडी = S. अ+ल
def: s.f. A thump, buffet, rap, slap, box
subs: s.f. or dhaul-dhappā, s.m. Thumping and slapping, mutual cuffing, a fight, row
v.n. To get a thump or buffet; to receive a blow or shock, to suffer loss

def: s.m. A kind of sugar-cane; the stalk of joʼār (which is sucked as sugar-cane is).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), White, clean, bright; — the colour white; a white ox or bull; any white thing (as milk, &c.); a kind of sugar-cane, &c. ( = dhaul, q.v.)
subs: v.n. To turn white or pale; — dhaulā-giri, dhaulā-gir, s.m. Name of one of the highest peaks of the snowy range of the Himālayas ( = dhavala-giri).

ety: dhaulā, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Whiteness, cleanness, brightness.

ety: dhaul, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To thump, slap, buffet, cuff ( = dhaul mārnā).

ety: dhaulā, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Whiteness, &c. ( = dhaulāʼī, q.v.).
v.t. = dhaulānā, q.v.
conj. Whether.
adj. & s.m. = ḍhām & ḍhāmā, q.v.
s.m. A weight of half a man or twenty seers (ser).
s.f. Thought, consideration, reflection (syn. dhyān).
s.f. Blowing, puffing; breathing, panting; — the asthma.
s.f. Bellows ( = dhauṅknī).

ety: dhauṅk˚ = Ap. Prk. धवँक्क(इ), Prk. धमक्के(इ), fr. S. धम (rt.ध्मा+कृ
def: v.t. To blow (with bellows, &c.); to breathe upon; — v.n. To blow, to puff, pant.
s.f. Blowing, panting, hard breathing; — bellows
subs: (-ko), To suffer from shortness of breath, to be blown; to puff, pant, breathe hard.

def: s.f. Noisy demeanour, overbearing conduct, bluster; impetuous assault; threatening, menace; imposition, exaction, extortion; deception, trick, cheating; — s.m. A large kettle-drum
subs: s.m. Blustering, bullying, tumult; impetuous assault (of a crowd or body of men)
(-ko), To threaten; to deceive, trick, cheat
(-kī), To be influenced by a threat; — to be taken in, be tricked or cheated.

def: s.m. Boldness, daringness, presumption; power, ability; attack, assault; shock, blow, injury, misfortune; — a large keetle-drum
subs: v.n. To suffer a shock or blow, to be unfortunate or ill-fated.

def: s.m. One who conducts himself audaciously or overbearingly; the leader of an assault, the leader of a hue and cry or posse comitatus; — an extortioner; a trickster, a cheat.
adj. Rich, wealthy; profuse, lavish, open-handed, unsparing; — stout, strong; bold, brave; brisk, smart, active, sharp; loose, disorderly, licentious; vicious, mischievous, wicked.

ety: dhauṅtāl, q.v.+ī = āʼī = Prk.अइआ = S.aff. ता+इका; — and -(adj.) Prk. इओ = S. इकः
def: s.f. Riches; profuseness, lavishness; strength; courage, &c. (see dhauṅtāl); — adj. = dhauṅtāl.
s.m. A large species of dove; — a kind of sugar-cane.
adj. White, &c. ( = dhaulā, q.v.).
adj. Of or pertaining to a cheat; cheating, dishonest, roguish, knavish, fraudulent, &c.
part. Washed, cleansed, purified; bleached; polished, bright — dhauta-śila, s.m. 'Bright stone'; crystal, rock-crystal.
